seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: composite: retheme to a two-stage festival layout and generalize the balance rule from exact equal-size bisection to a node-weighted (crowd-draw) balance with a tolerance tau, keeping the max-cut objective; the node weights plus a planted-but-noisy community structure make the balanced optimum NP-hard and unknown, and let index-balancing, greedy placement, and flip/swap local search diverge.
title: Twin-Stage Festival Layout: Maximizing Cross-Stage Crowd Flow
strategies: index-balance baseline: walk performers in input order, always assign to the lighter stage (flow-blind) -> the calibration point
greedy flow-aware placement: order by incident flow, place each performer opposite its heavier-weighted placed neighbours, then repair balance
single-flip local search: repeatedly flip a performer whenever it raises cross-stage flow and keeps |T0-T1| <= tau
balance-preserving swap / Kernighan-Lin: exchange one performer per stage to escape flip-only local optima
randomized multi-restart: run local search from many balanced random starts and keep the best feasible layout
